epigraph.text: The most common of all follies is to believe passionately in the palpably not true.

follyOfTheDay.headline: Judge Orders Man to Apologize to Pickle Jar
follyOfTheDay.summary: In a ruling that transcends conventional jurisprudence, a municipal court judge has mandated that a defendant issue a formal apology to a pickle jar—the erstwhile object of his destructive rage. The defendant, apparently overcome with ire, had committed some manner of affront upon the innocent condiment vessel, prompting the court to intervene with this deliciously absurd remedy. Legal precedent being what it is, the judge deemed simple restitution insufficient; only performative contrition to the inanimate object itself would satisfy justice. The man dutifully appeared and tendered his apologies to the jar, which by all accounts received them with the stoic indifference one might expect from a pickle jar. The ruling stands as yet another monument to the boundless creativity of the American judiciary in manufacturing novel punishments for human stupidity.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: The judiciary has spoken, and the pickle jar has witnessed humiliation enough for one lifetime.
